source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Mother halves the birthday cake. One half she then halves again. Of that she again halves one of the smaller pieces. Of these smaller pieces she once more halves one of them (see diagram). One of the two smallest pieces weighs $100 \mathrm{~g}$.
How much does the entire cake weigh?
<image1>

image_dependency: however, the halving sequence is also described in the text, so the image may aid understanding more than provide unique necessary information
multi_step: solving requires tracking several successive halving operations across different pieces
requires combining fraction reasoning with identification of the final smallest piece size before scaling from 100 g to whole-cake weight
multiple-choice options do not remove the need for sequential reasoning
verifiability: answer is a single numeric quantity with unit ($1600 g$)
the reasoning path is checkable by explicit fraction progression (down to a smallest piece fraction, then invert)

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和图像资产齐全，registry_passed=true，且无登记问题
综合初评分较高：multi_step_score=0.84，verifiability_score=0.94，具有较好的推理价值与可核验性
虽有“diagram referenced”风险标记，但证据表明核心结构在文本中也有描述，不构成核心语义缺失
